revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:千形神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:拝殿（正面）.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Kumagaya|qid=Q41106|1=Kumagaya}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.145791666667|N|139.38475277778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}
'''Chikata Shrine''' (千形神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kumagaya|en|Kumagaya|qid=Q41106|lt=Kumagaya City|1=Kumagaya}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. The shrine's former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was that of a village shrine. == Deity == ; Main Shrine * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|en|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}, worshipped as 天津彦火々瓊々杵命<ref name="Kumagaya City History Later Volume">{{Cite book|和書|author=Kumagaya City History Compilation Committee|title=Kumagaya City Regional History Encyclopedia|publisher=Kumagaya City|year=1964|page=310|qq=}}</ref>, also known as 天津彦火瓊杵々杵尊 (Amatsu Hiko Hono Ninigi)<ref name="Saitama Shrine Research">{{Cite book|和書|author=Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Team|title=Shrines of Saitama: Ōsato, Kitakatsushika, Hiki|publisher=Kumagaya City|year=1964|page=78|qq=}}</ref>. ; Auxiliary Shrines * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} * {{ill|Futodama|qid=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|ja|天太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ|1=Futodama}}<ref name="Kumagaya City History Later Volume"/> == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown<ref name="Ōsato District Local History 194">{{Cite book|和書|author=Edited by Etō Shimoda|title=Ōsato District Local History|publisher=Saitama Minpōsha|year=1919|page=46|qq=}}</ref>, but its origins are said to trace back to the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}<ref name="Local Encyclopedia">{{Cite book|和書|author=Kumagaya City Local Culture Association|title=Kumagaya City Local History Encyclopedia|publisher=Kumagaya City Local Culture Association|year=1979|page=46|qq=}}</ref>. According to legend, a giant {{ill|bear|ja|熊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bear|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クマ|1=Bear|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11788}} terrorized the area, and a man named Fujiwara no Chikata rallied villagers to hunt and kill it<ref name="Local Encyclopedia"/>. The bear was eventually slain in {{ill|Higashimatsuyama, Saitama|en|Higashimatsuyama, Saitama|qid=Q823907|lt=Higashimatsuyama|1=Higashimatsuyama, Saitama}}, and the bear's spirit was enshrined as Akakuma Myōjin (Red Bear Deity)<ref name="Local Encyclopedia"/>. Over time, the shrine became known as Chikata Shrine<ref name="Local Encyclopedia"/>. An alternate legend attributes the bear's defeat to Taira no Naosada (father of {{ill|Kumagai Naozane|en|Kumagai Naozane|qid=Q3335867|lt=Kumagai Naozane|1=Kumagai Naozane}}), who supposedly built a shrine on the site of the bear's death to appease its spirit<ref name="Guide Map">{{Cite web|和書|author=Kumagaya Volunteer Guides|title=Nakasendō Kumagaya Post Town Tourism Guide Map|publisher=7th Edition, 2014|qq=}}</ref>. However, this version is considered an embellished tale promoted by the Kumagai clan<ref name="Local Encyclopedia"/>. From the Hōreki era (1751–1764), the shrine hosted sumo wrestling events annually in early October, earning it recognition as the birthplace of sumo in Kumagaya<ref name="Local Encyclopedia"/><ref name="Digital Museum">{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/nakasendou/25_b12.htm|title=Nakasendō and Historical Sites|publisher=Kumagaya Municipal Cultural Heritage Center|access-date=2018-11-25|qq=}}</ref>. == Grounds == The shrine features a {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|qid=Q234731|lt=torii|1=Torii}} donated in 1971, flanked by stone lanterns from 1913<ref name="Kumagaya City Cultural Society">{{Cite book|和書|author=Shibayama Kōnosuke|title=Kumagaya City Local Cultural Society Journal: Old City Edition|publisher=Kumagaya City Cultural Society|year=1977|pages=20-21|qq=}}</ref>. A Kagura Hall, sumo ring remnants, and auxiliary shrines are also present. === Auxiliary Shrines === * Inari Shrine<ref name="Shrines of Saitama"/> == Location == ; A11ress * 1-18 Honmachi, Kumagaya City, Saitama Prefecture, Japan ; Access * Approx. 13 minutes on foot northeast of {{ill|Kumagaya Station|en|Kumagaya Station|qid=Q735594|lt=Kumagaya Station|1=Kumagaya Station}}. == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/kounanmatinoiseki/p12naozanemap.pdf Map of Sites Related to Kumagai Naozane – Kumagaya City Cultural Heritage Center] * [https://www.kumagayakan.net/bk/sp/0412_4.html Touring Kumagaya's Landmarks – Kumagaya Net] {{神道 横|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|千形神社|version=97926401|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:千形神社]]
<!--Chikagata Shrine--><!--Chikagata Shrine--><!--Chikagata Shrine--><!--Chikagata Shrine--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Chikata-jinja (Kumagaya, Saitama)}} {{wikidata link|Q59282642}} [[Category:Son-sha in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kumagaya]] [[Category:熊谷市の歴史]]
